content.full_text:
April 18, 1990

Mr. Kevin Braaten-Moen  
Technical Assistance Division  
California Fair Political Practices Commission  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Braaten-Moen:

I am writing to confirm my understanding of instructions received from you by telephone today.

My question dealt with a candidate in a California special State Senate election who received a contribution from a California Major Donor Committee too late for use in that special election. Instead, the candidate, an incumbent member of the California State Assembly, notified the major donor committee that the contribution would be deposited into the account for re-election to the Assembly.

In its year-end 1989 disclosure report, prior to learning from the candidate that the donation would be re-designated, the major donor committee listed this contribution as a donation to the special Senate race. My question was what amendment procedures were now required of the major donor committee, in as much as that contribution was instead used for the candidate’s re-election to the State Assembly.

Your response indicated that if the major donor committee had indeed intended the donation to be used for the Senate race, had issued the check with that designation, and had reported the contribution in the same manner, then no amendment from the major donor committee was necessary, even though the candidate used the donation for another election.

Thank you very much for your assistance in this matter. If I have not understood your directions clearly, please contact me at (703) 549-7705.

Sincerely:

Keith A. Davis
